message:
feat(startup_checks): enrich PID hint files with executable path for robust process identity

Previously, the listener PID hint file stored only the numeric PID.
When checking whether the port holder was Agent Mail, the code relied
solely on pid_is_agent_mail() which inspects /proc/<pid>/exe (Linux)
or ps output (macOS) for an agent-mail signature in the binary name.
This could fail if the binary was renamed, installed to an unusual
path, or if PID recycling occurred.

Now the hint file stores both the PID and the canonical path to the
executable that wrote it (e.g. "/usr/local/bin/mcp-agent-mail").
When validating the port holder, hinted_pid_matches_listener() first
checks that the PID is actually among the port's listeners, then
verifies identity via either:
  1. Path comparison: the running process's exe path matches the
     recorded path (via canonicalize on both sides), OR
  2. Name signature: the traditional pid_is_agent_mail() name check.

Key changes:
- Add ListenerPidHint struct with pid and optional exe_path fields
- write_listener_pid_hint() now serializes PID + exe path (2 lines)
- parse_listener_pid_hint() / read_listener_pid_hint() deserialize
  the enriched format (backward-compatible: old single-line files
  parse as hint with exe_path=None)
- Extract pid_executable_path() from pid_executable_basename() on
  both Linux and non-Linux, returning Option<PathBuf>
- Non-Linux pid_executable_path() now uses `ps -o command=` instead
  of `ps -o comm=` and extracts argv0 to get the full path rather
  than just the basename
- Unify hinted_agent_mail_pid() across platforms using the new
  hinted_pid_matches_listener() helper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
